metadata.title: Next-Generation Gene Therapy for Parkinson's Disease Using Engineered Viral Vectors.
metadata.text: This review summarizes the development and their potential in treating Parkinson's disease (PD).

metadata.title: Levodopa versus levodopa sparing in early parkinson's disease: can we meet halfway?
metadata.text: Monotherapy is the recommended initial treatment for early Parkinson's disease.

metadata.title: Levodopa-Induced Dyskinesia in Parkinson's Disease: Pathogenesis and Emerging Treatment Strategies.
metadata.text: The most commonly used treatment for Parkinson's disease (PD) is levodopa, prescribed in conjunction with carbidopa.

metadata.title: Ketogenic Diets for Symptoms of Parkinson's Disease
metadata.text: Current treatments for Parkinson's disease (including levodopa and deep brain stimulation) improve certain symptoms but are not thought to improve the underlying neurodegenerative disease process (they are not a "cure").

metadata.title: Parkinson's disease--levodopa complications.
metadata.text: This article reviews the current management of late-stage Parkinson's disease.

metadata.title: [Parkinson's Disease: Clinical Review and Update].
metadata.text: This paper presents an updated review on Parkinson's disease, guiding the readership through current concepts, and allowing their application to daily clinical practice.

metadata.title: Levodopa-Induced Dyskinesia in Parkinson's Disease: Pathogenesis and Emerging Treatment Strategies.
metadata.text: Levodopa replacement therapy remains the current clinical treatment of choice for Parkinson's patients, but approximately 80% of the treated PD patients develop levodopa-induced dyskinesia (LID) in the advanced stages of the disease.

metadata.title: TMS Treatment in Parkinson's Disease With Pain.
metadata.text: The study intends to use this technique to treat such a disabling symptom in patients who suffer from Parkinson's Disease (PD).

metadata.title: The treatment of early Parkinson's disease: levodopa rehabilitated.
metadata.text: Levodopa remains the most effective and best tolerated Parkinson's drug to date, and should have an important role in all therapeutic strategies, both as monotherapy in early Parkinson's disease and as part of combination therapy in advanced disease.

metadata.title: Treatment of Motor Symptoms of Parkinson's Disease.
metadata.text: The most effective pharmacologic intervention used to treat motor symptoms in Parkinson's disease is levodopa, which is available in various formulations, including newer continuous subcutaneous infusions.

metadata.title: Levodopa treatment: impacts and mechanisms throughout Parkinson's disease progression.
metadata.text: Treatment with levodopa, a precursor of dopamine (DA), to compensate for the loss of endogenous DA in Parkinson's disease (PD), has been a success story for over 50 years.

metadata.title: The Treatment of Older Patients with Parkinson's Disease.
metadata.text: In this narrative review, we propose practical ways to simplify the treatment regimen of older patients with Parkinson's disease to lessen side effects and make treatment more manageable for these patients and the persons caring for them.

metadata.title: Impact of Physical Exercise on Levodopa Therapy Across Parkinson's Disease Stages.
metadata.text: Levodopa is the gold standard of treatment in Parkinson's disease (PD).

metadata.title: Recent developments in gene therapy for Parkinson's disease.
metadata.text: Parkinson's disease (PD) is a progressive, neurodegenerative disorder for which there is currently no cure.

metadata.title: Rehabilitation for Parkinson's disease: Current outlook and future challenges.
metadata.text: Rehabilitation is considered as an adjuvant to pharmacological and surgical treatments for Parkinson's disease (PD) to maximize functional ability and minimize secondary complications.

metadata.title: Progress towards therapies for disease modification in Parkinson's disease.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't, Review
metadata.text: The development of interventions to slow or halt the progression of Parkinson's disease remains a priority for patients and researchers alike.

metadata.title: Model-based optimization of controlled release formulation of levodopa for Parkinson's disease.
metadata.text: Levodopa is currently the standard of care treatment for Parkinson's disease, but chronic therapy has been linked to motor complications.

metadata.title: Medical Management and Prevention of Motor Complications in Parkinson's Disease.
metadata.text: Levodopa is the most effective medication for the treatment of the motor symptoms of Parkinson's disease.

metadata.title: Telephone-Based Counseling for Depression in Parkinson's Disease
metadata.text: There is a critical need for treatments that address depression and barriers to mental health care in Parkinson's disease (PD).

metadata.title: A Study to Investigate a New Treatment in Patients With Parkinson's Disease
metadata.text: The purpose of this clinical trial conducted in patients with Parkinson's Disease is to study the relationship between patient individual profile and their response to IPT803 Adjunct Treatment (treatment response being characterized by movements improvement).

metadata.title: The European Physiotherapy Guideline for Parkinson's Disease: Implications for Neurologists.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't
metadata.text: As such, this paper offers very practical guidance for clinicians working with Parkinson's disease patients and who consider physiotherapy treatments for their patients.

metadata.title: Deep Brain Stimulation and Nonmotor Symptoms.
metadata.text: Deep brain stimulation (DBS) is currently the treatment of choice for advanced Parkinson's disease (PD).

metadata.title: Non-motor symptoms of Parkinson's disease: diagnosis and management.
metadata.text: Inevitably, the development of treatments that can slow or prevent the progression of Parkinson's disease and its multicentric neurodegeneration provides the best hope of curing non-motor symptoms.

metadata.title: Programming Deep Brain Stimulation for Parkinson's Disease: The Toronto Western Hospital Algorithms.
metadata.text: Deep brain stimulation (DBS) is an established and effective treatment for Parkinson's disease (PD).

metadata.title: The treatment of early Parkinson's disease: levodopa rehabilitated.
metadata.text: Many clinicians regard levodopa as a last resort in the symptomatic treatment of Parkinson's disease.

metadata.title: Circuit-specific gene therapy reverses core symptoms in a primate Parkinson's disease model.

metadata.text: Parkinson's disease (PD) is a debilitating neurodegenerative disorder.

metadata.text: Parkinson's Disease (PD) is an intractable disease resulting in localized neurodegeneration of dopaminergic neurons of the substantia nigra pars compacta.

metadata.title: A Placebo- and Active Controlled Study of Preladenant in Subjects With Moderate to Severe Parkinson's Disease (P04938)
metadata.text: When a patient with Parkinson's disease (PD) is initially treated with L-dopa or dopamine agonists, the symptoms of PD improve or disappear.

metadata.title: Stroop Test for Parkinson's Disease with Deep Brain Stimulation: A Systematic Review.
metadata.text: Deep brain stimulation (DBS) is considered an alternative treatment for patients with rapidly developing Parkinson's disease (PD).

metadata.text: Deep brain stimulation (DBS) has become an effective and safe treatment in patients with Parkinson's disease (PD) not responding to conventional treatments.

metadata.title: A Study of LY4006896 in Healthy Participants and Participants With Parkinson's Disease
metadata.text: The treatment and follow-up duration will be up to 61 weeks for participants with Parkinson's disease, and 48 weeks for healthy participants.

metadata.text: STN-DBS is a cornerstone in the treatment of advanced Parkinson's disease (PD).

metadata.text: After the introduction of levodopa, deep brain stimulation emerged as the second therapy with an important symptomatic impact in the treatment of Parkinson's disease.

metadata.title: Deep brain stimulation in Parkinson's disease: A scientometric and bibliometric analysis, trends, and research hotspots.
metadata.text: Parkinson disease (PD), a prevalent neurodegenerative ailment in the elderly, relies mainly on pharmacotherapy, yet deep brain stimulation (DBS) emerges as a vital remedy for refractory cases.

metadata.title: Long-term neuropsychological outcomes of deep brain stimulation in early-stage Parkinson's disease.
metadata.study_type: Journal Article, Randomized Controlled Trial, Research Support, N.I.H., Extramural, Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't
metadata.text: The pilot trial of deep brain stimulation (DBS) in early-stage Parkinson's disease (PD) randomized 30 patients (medication duration 0.5-4 years; without dyskinesia or motor fluctuations) to receive optimal drug therapy alone (early ODT) or subthalamic nucleus (STN) DBS plus ODT (early DBS + ODT).
